note: Accepted PRODUCTION (non-test/fixture) sharded≡exact RESOLVED-edge divergences on this repo, plus the downstream SCC-membership divergence count. functionsOnly{Exact,Sharded} MUST be 0 (a hard failure). A run EXCEEDING either number fails graph-equivalence-check (a resolver regression). This is a ratchet: a decrease passes and prints a hint to tighten the number. RESIDUAL CHARACTERIZATION (graph-engine-convergence diagnosis): the 204 splits ~110 'exact=[] sharded=[hash]' + ~81 'exact=[hash] sharded=[]' + a few SCC-only. The 110 are NOT exact under-resolution: ~61 are sharded OVER-resolving a same-file/same-package simple-name (e.g. logger.info -> the caller's own info method; this.inner.clear -> a same-file method) which exact CORRECTLY declines, ~36 are column-misaligned DUPLICATES of an edge exact already has (same target, off-by-one column from boundary extraction), and ~1 cross-pkg phantom. The 81 ARE a real exact-superiority class: cross-package calls routed through a re-export chain (export { x } from './y') that exact's type checker follows but the sharded export-index linker declines (e.g. childrenOf/namedChildrenOf re-exported by graph-adapter-common from tree-sitter; isTestFile/isCommentLine re-exported through the fitness barrel). Closing the 81 needs walk-time re-export capture + an extends-following export index (deferred — a resolution-format change that would alter findings). The STRUCTURAL (unresolved-vs-absent) partition is now 0 (was 1615) after pruning empty-to cross-shard placeholders.
